item_spec.concrete_change:
Make the canonical conversation-first dispatch path acquire the SAME live-quota inputs the legacy in-process path already builds (cli.ts:2030-2056), then thread them into scheduleWave. Precise gap (verified against source): prepareDispatchArtifacts (dispatch.ts:640-660) ALREADY reads readQuotaState/quotaStateEntry, lookupDiscoveredLimits (cache), resolveHostActiveSubagentLimit, and calls scheduleWave with discoveredLimits — so it is not 'no quota', it is missing exactly three rungs: (1) provider.queryLimits, (2) the real-time QuotaSource snapshot, and (3) a real hostModel. Changes: 

(A) In prepareDispatchArtifacts (packages/audit-code/src/cli/dispatch.ts, the block at ~640-660): after computing quotaProviderKey, (i) optionally query provider limits via a new injected param `queryLimits?: (model: string | null) => Promise<DiscoveredRateLimits | null>` — `const providerLimits = (await params.queryLimits?.(hostModel).then((r) => (r ? { ...r, source: "provider_query" } : null)).catch(() => null)) ?? null;` (ii) merge ahead of cache: `const discoveredLimits = mergeDiscoveredLimits(providerLimits, dispatchCachedLimits);` (replacing the current `discoveredLimits: dispatchCachedLimits`); (iii) build the real-time source and snapshot exactly like the legacy path: `const halfLifeHours = sessionConfig.quota?.empirical_half_life_hours ?? 24; const quotaSource = new CompositeQuotaSource([new LearnedQuotaSource(halfLifeHours)]); const quotaSourceSnapshot = await quotaSource.queryCurrentUsage(quotaProviderKey).catch(() => null);` (iv) pass `quotaSourceSnapshot` into the scheduleWave({...}) call. New imports in dispatch.ts: CompositeQuotaSource, LearnedQuotaSource (from @audit-tools/shared) and mergeDiscoveredLimits (from ../quota/discoveredLimits.js); lookupDiscoveredLimits/mergeDiscoveredLimits/scheduleWave/buildProviderModelKey/readQuotaState/resolveHostActiveSubagentLimit/resolveFreshSessionProviderName are already imported. Because scheduleWave already returns quota_source_snapshot (scheduler.ts:191) and DispatchQuota already surfaces it (dispatch.ts:672), no scheduler.ts or quotaSource.ts change is needed — once the snapshot is passed, scheduler.ts:168-177 becomes reachable and dispatch-quota.json.quota_source_snapshot stops being null. discoveredLimits.ts needs no change (lookupDiscoveredLimits/mergeDiscoveredLimits already exist). 

(B) In cmdPrepareDispatch (packages/audit-code/src/cli.ts): construct the resolved provider from sessionConfig (createFreshSessionProvider) and pass `queryLimits: provider.queryLimits?.bind(provider)` plus a resolved `hostModel` (best-effort: from session-config quota model hint / provider / env; fall back to null) into prepareDispatchArtifacts, instead of the current hostModel:null with no queryLimits. 

Layering notes: provider.queryLimits is implemented by zero providers today (FINDING-003), so the provider-query rung returns null until that lands — wiring it now is correct and degrades gracefully. The hostModel resolution source ties into the model-based rung of the cascade (FINDING-004) and may be refined there. Keep graceful degradation: every new await is wrapped in .catch(()=>null) so a first-contact run with no signal still produces today's safe default without throwing. Do NOT change the legacy path; this only brings the canonical path to parity. To keep prepareDispatchArtifacts unit-testable without spawning a provider, prefer the injected `queryLimits` param over constructing a provider inside the function; optionally extract the snapshot/limits acquisition into a small helper (e.g. acquireDispatchQuota(providerKey, sessionConfig, queryLimits)) for direct testing.

tests_to_write.name: canonical dispatch threads a real-time quota snapshot into wave scheduling
assertions: When the quota-acquisition path yields a snapshot with remaining_pct < 0.1, the resulting wave_size collapses to 1 and cooldown_until is set from the snapshot's reset_at (scheduler.ts:168-173 becomes reachable).
When remaining_pct is between 0.1 and 0.3, the wave_size is at least halved versus the no-snapshot result.
The computed quota_source_snapshot is non-null and is reflected in the DispatchQuota output (dispatch-quota.json.quota_source_snapshot), not the current hard null.

tests_to_write.name: canonical dispatch merges provider-queried limits ahead of the on-disk cache
assertions: When an injected queryLimits returns rate limits, they are merged via mergeDiscoveredLimits with source 'provider_query' taking precedence over cached values for fields it provides.
When queryLimits is absent or rejects, the path falls back to lookupDiscoveredLimits (cache) exactly as today (no throw).

tests_to_write.name: canonical dispatch degrades gracefully on first contact with no quota signal
assertions: With no provider queryLimits, no cached limits, and an empty learned quota state, prepareDispatchArtifacts still completes and emits a valid dispatch-quota.json with source 'default' / confidence 'low' (today's safe behavior is preserved).
No unhandled rejection is produced when readQuotaState, lookupDiscoveredLimits, queryLimits, or queryCurrentUsage reject.
